skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
test_name: June 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: A market researcher collected data on the price of a hot dog at 59 locations selected at random in a state. The mean price of a hot dog in the state was estimated to be \$3.28, with an associated margin of error of \$0.06. Which of the following is a plausible statement about the mean price of a hot dog for all locations that sell this product in the state?

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is either less than \$3.22 or greater than \$3.34.

test_name: June 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 2
passage: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 7 kilograms traveling at a speed of $v$ meters per second is given by the function $K$, where $K(v)= \frac{7}{2} v^2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $K(24)= 2,016$ in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The object traveling at 240 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The object traveling at 2,016 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The object traveling at 7,056 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.
